user_prompt: Can you summarize Article 28 in 3 bullet points?
actual_output:
# Article 28 (Data Processing) — 3-Point Summary

• **Processor Obligation:** Any natural or legal person processing personal data on behalf of a controller must have a contract (Data Processing Agreement) that binds them to GDPR obligations, including confidentiality, security measures, and sub-processor authorization.

• **Controller Responsibility:** Controllers remain liable for processor compliance. You must ensure processors implement appropriate technical and organizational measures (Article 32) and only process data on your documented instructions.

• **Sub-processor Rules:** Processors cannot engage sub-processors without prior specific or general authorization from the controller. Any sub-processor change requires advance notice and the right to object.

---

**Practical takeaway:** If you use *any* third party to handle EU user data (cloud hosting, email service, analytics platform, payment processor), you need a signed DPA in place. This is one of the most commonly missed compliance requirements.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly and accurately fulfilled the user's request to summarize Article 28 in three bullet points while maintaining its persona.
